summary: §4.2.2 rewrite leading with dome's misstatement of standard-physics eclipse prediction (claims globe predicts 0 nT). Adds two leading paragraphs with verbatim dome quotes (live-power Domain 11 falsification + eclipse countdown banner), demotes existing Chapman 1933 paragraph to support, closes with BOU-2017-already-falsifies hammer linking to §4.2.5. Secondary patch: one-sentence cross-reference at head of §4.2.3.

backward: §4.2.3 opening sentence → §4.2.2 (dependency on misstatement framing — secondary_patch)

curmudgeon_recheck_list: Verify dome quotes still present at curmudgeon review time (live-power Domain 11 + countdown banner)
Test whether "globe predicts 0" framing is universal across dome eclipse material or limited to live-power Domain 11 — if limited, weaken framing accordingly

Test BOU-already-falsifies hammer for over-reach (dome may argue BOU 2017 was "globe-era" baseline and rule applies only to 2026; the trilemma in the closing paragraph already covers this, but curmudgeon should confirm)
Optional: cross-check −10.9 nT figure against NOAA/USGS Boulder primary source — currently relies on existing §4.2.5 framing
hnote_consumption_status: HNOTE-OPERATOR-4-2-2-MISSTATEMENT-LEAD-001 → status remains "pending" until decider integrates EXP-253 to data/sections.json. Per existing convention (HNOTE-001 / HNOTE-002 / HNOTE-003 pattern across prior runs), the analyst delivers the EXP and the decider flips HNOTE status to "consumed" on integration.
thinking_about_other_side: Per user-preferences directive (consider opposing arguments): a defender of the dome could argue the "globe predicts 0 nT" framing is reader-shorthand for "globe predicts no eclipse-specific anomaly above background" — i.e. the residual after the dome's own subtraction, not a literal claim about Chapman. The closing trilemma in §4.2.2 covers this: if the dome retreats to "we meant something else by 0", it concedes the rule was not operational in the published form, which is the second horn (revise criterion → concede non-discriminating). The argument is robust to charitable reinterpretation. A second defender argument (BOU 2017 was a different eclipse under different conditions, the rule applies to 2026 only) is also covered by the trilemma — if the rule's domain was unspecified in advance, the dome concedes the rule was not pre-registered.
